Cobalt Helm  
 

Lore Item
Slot: HEAD
AC: 25
Effect: Serpent Sight (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 10.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR
Race: ALL except IKS
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Cobalt Helm
Lore Item
Slot: HEAD
AC: 25
Effect: Serpent Sight (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 10.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR
Race: ALL except IKS
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Plate
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):50, 50, 120
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:35 pp 0 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:910
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-17 02:25:17
Page Updated:Sun Nov 13th, 2016

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 1,500pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Old Sebilis
NPC Name
froglok reet knight
froglok reet shaman
froglok reet wizard
sebilite juggernaut

Ssraeshza Temple
NPC Name
Advisor Zekuzh
Arbiter Korazhk


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Old Sebilis
Ssraeshza Temple

Cobalt Helm item window 2016

Uploaded October 24th, 2016 by snailish
Send a correction
Post Comment
Dropped in Ssraeshza Temple
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2007 at 6:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591923873379
Scholar
319 posts
I was on a raid for the High Priest in Ssraeshza Temple. They cleared the first room where all the golems and Chapel Guards are, and then the room with the high priest by pulling all of them to the room with the captured thought leech. I was rapidly sorting through the bodies, and found this on a corpse. My recollection was the helm came from a corpse, and not from something someone dropped on the ground. I am certain of it because after I looted it, I checked what the Cobalt Helm was by cliking on the link in the box which said I looted it. I thought it might be a little out of place, so I checked here. I had a power failure, and when I came back I promptly died to all the pops. Curiously the Cobalt Helm was on my cursor as I zoned in (I am almost certain I put it in my inventory before the power failure), and the Cobalt Helm was still on my cursor when I zoned into the guild lobby. I suspect there must be a bug here. Too bad it wasn't something really useful, like a piece of Solaris gear. Which mob did it come from? I have no clue, but there was the usual collection of snakes, live, undead, and golems. All of them were the trash mobs. The HP had not yet been engaged when I looted it.
▲ Page top
Dropped in Ssraeshza Temple
QuoteReply
# Nov 04 2007 at 6:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591923873379
Scholar
319 posts
Dropped again today from Advisor Zekuzh while getting ready for a raid for the Emperor Ssraeshza. So this is the second time it dropped on the third floor.

__DEL__1591788756993
26 posts
It's stupid if a warrior has to worry about weight. My warrior has a current 180 str and i need to work on it. Well maybe it's cuz i have 2 rallic paks and a bag of tinkerers, but still warriors shouldn't have problems with weight and another thing have you ever heard of a BANK!?

Cobalt Helmet
QuoteReply
# Mar 31 2002 at 6:20 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591775021136
10 posts
Bottom line, this helmet has 25AC. You tell me of any other helmet that is easier to get with as much or more AC than that. Quit ******** about the effect and who needs it more. ***** human and Barb warriors, im a Dark Elf warrior with ultra vision and wear this helmet because it has 25AC!!!

# Jan 12 2004 at 5:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789965084

65warrior Asatru
povar
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 08 2003 at 3:59 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) geek, buy a helm of raldukan or tae ew war helm. tcobalt helm sucks. you want ease? Buy it. whats easyer than that. 25raw ac and nuthing else SUX.
RE: Cobalt Helmet
QuoteReply
# Feb 13 2003 at 10:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763363016
15 posts
Just beacause something has no other stat adds does not make any less worth having. 25 raw AC is very nice upgrade for a warrior as they are moving through the levels especially if they do not have the moeny to buy such items and happen to loot it or have it handed to them from a helpful PC. A Warriors Main concerns are AC and HP ( Straight AC or STA ), then STR, after that it can be argued all day long.


http://www.magelo.com/eq_view_profile.html?num=543642

B) Can it be cast on others as well?

C) Out of curiosity, cast time?

QuoteReply
# Dec 04 2001 at 8:12 AM Rating: Default

2 posts
Wow ..What a shame that only 2 people here had anything good to say about this item...And yes just like the others Im opening my trap for nothing less than to sound dumb..Great helm ..
▲ Page top
Think about it!
QuoteReply
# Nov 16 2001 at 12:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760216655
1 post
I think you are all missing the point of this helm. This is pretty much only usefull for 2 races... Human and Barbarian... 2 races that are strong enough to not notice the weight and 2 races that have terrible vision.
I personally use 3 helms, 1: Indocolite Helmet(PoH) great for leveling skills 2: Cobalt Helmet(sebilis) great for traveling and dungeons 3: Custom Champions Crown(Thurg Quest) great for when you need those extra stats.

No i do not carry all 3 around... if my skills are maxed out i leave the indo helm in the bank... if im hangin with a shammy then the cobby helm gets put on the shelf too.

Think about it

53 champion
Tunare
▲ Page top
RE: Think about it!
QuoteReply
# Jul 26 2002 at 2:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780563692
19 posts
Heh! This helm is 25ac! That makes it awesome for ANY warrior, it's not "pretty much useful for only humans and barbarians", though of course they would benefit from the effect. And the weight thing; any warrior high enough to acquire one of these babies will doubtlessly have at least 170str, usually a good bit more; plenty of str for MANY 10 weight items. But oh well, I came hoping for a low PC on it hehe.. sucks for me, 15k.
▲ Page top
Barb and Human Only?!?
QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2001 at 11:48 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361752160
5 posts
I agree barbs and humans probably do need it the most, considering their nightly vision (as well as depth). But one thing I found silly is that you said humans are like hella strong... /disagree Str-wise, the big races prevail base, but I'm little ole half-elf pushing over 200 str. (200 being base, and always have spells either druid/shammy or just my cobalt boots) I dunno why I'm replying, I guess I just wanted to poke fun at that humans being strong thing, and I do agree with your idea of having several helms... But I wouldn't mind a few extra with Cobalt, condering i still have a ssb. Plus, Half Elves have the best looking armor LOL

Illuminas Wins

QuoteReply
# May 31 2001 at 6:56 PM Rating: Default

if u are complaining about wieght and dont get ic because of that u are dumb, 25ac is sweet. thick banded belt is 15str if u dont have and fbss good option, and there tons of stuff wars get to enhance str. if u were a monk or caster that dont have many str enhancing items good arguemtn, but this is for a war that is sapposed to have str boosting stuff anyway, weghts no probs for war

Only Froglok Reets drop Cobalt helmet. Juggernauts drop Blood Ember helmet.

Cobalt Weights
QuoteReply
# Apr 20 2001 at 10:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248201018
5 posts
I understand about the weight concern (being a halfling warrior myself), but let's not forget that both the cobalt boots and cobalt gloves have strength-enhancing effects, which means that you should still be able to walk/run unecumbered even with a bunch of stuff in your inventory, assuming you've done your hunting in Karnor's (especially if you have weight-reduction bags, which you should).

wt
QuoteReply
# Apr 19 2001 at 12:59 PM Rating: Default

most warrior armour, including Velious quests, is heavy. They seem to think we are all Barbarians with 190 str. I'm a wood elf and I really wish they made some of this lighter.

QuoteReply
# Dec 10 2001 at 1:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361752160
5 posts
Ya, weight.... Umm, did you say you were a warrior? Dunno bout you, but str and sta are a warrior's best melee stats, and str = weight-carrying ability. You said a 190str Barb, well I am a Half Elf with a tad over 200 str, so don't say that it's a big-race item because it's heavy.. It's heavy cause it's a warrior item, silly. BTW, Half Elves have the ebst looking armor, WOOT!
